candidates.name: SonicMasterDataset
candidates.scaleSummary: Approximately 166k paired degraded/high-quality music rows; about 506 GB; 44.1 kHz examples visible.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Input and target FLAC fields are paired; source identity must be audited before split use.

candidates.labelingType: Paired degraded/high-quality audio with degradation metadata, prompts, genre, and quality metadata.
requiredFollowUps: Audit source IDs and Jamendo-derived provenance before ingestion.
Capture item-level attribution and checksum records.
Hold out original source works across train, validation, and test.

candidates.name: Cambridge-MT Mixing Secrets Library
candidates.scaleSummary: 625 projects; 16/24-bit WAV at 44.1 kHz; includes many mastering-tagged projects.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Project files and unmastered mixes require per-project selection and manual verification.

candidates.labelingType: Raw multitracks, mixes, previews, and some unmastered mix WAVs.
requiredFollowUps: Keep all use non-commercial unless copyright-holder permission is obtained.
Track project-level rights and contributor metadata.
Deduplicate against MUSDB18-HQ, MedleyDB, and SolidStateBusComp.

candidates.name: SolidStateBusComp / Diff-SSL-G-Comp
candidates.scaleSummary: 175 unmastered songs, 220 parameter combinations, about 2.5k hours, about 4.8 TB.

audioFormat.encoding: audio files from gated Hugging Face dataset
audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Same sources repeated across many compressor settings; split by source song only.

candidates.labelingType: Input/output pairs from SSL bus-compressor processing.
requiredFollowUps: Use only for dynamics/compressor subtask research.
Respect CC BY-NC and Cambridge-derived constraints.
Group all parameter sweeps from one source song into the same split.

candidates.name: MUSDB18-HQ
candidates.scaleSummary: 150 full-length tracks, about 10 hours, stereo 44.1 kHz, 22.7 GB HQ WAV.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Use original train/test split and deduplicate upstream project overlap.

candidates.labelingType: Mixture plus drums, bass, vocals, and other stems.
requiredFollowUps: Capture per-track license and academic access status.
Deduplicate with Cambridge-MT and MedleyDB origins.
Use stem synthesis rather than treating mixture as verified mastered target.

candidates.name: MedleyDB 2.0
candidates.scaleSummary: 196 multitracks with raw audio, processed stems, mix files, metadata, genre labels, and annotations.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Mixes may or may not be mastered; mastered status is not labeled.

candidates.labelingType: Raw audio, processed stems, mix hierarchy, metadata, and annotations.
requiredFollowUps: Keep non-commercial/share-alike restrictions isolated.
Split by song and artist.
Use controlled synthetic targets rather than direct mastered labels.

candidates.name: MoisesDB
candidates.scaleSummary: Public multitrack/stem dataset with about 14 hours reported in secondary literature.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Stem taxonomy and bleed metadata must be normalized before synthesis.

candidates.labelingType: Hierarchical stems, mixture, genre, activity, and bleed metadata.
requiredFollowUps: Respect CC BY-NC-SA restrictions.
Normalize stem taxonomy before pair generation.
Keep source-level split isolation.

candidates.name: Open Multitrack Testbed
candidates.scaleSummary: Described as a large and diverse open multitrack repository with metadata.

audioFormat.channels: multitrack, mixes, and processed versions where available
audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Current catalog access and item-level licenses must be verified.

candidates.labelingType: Multitrack audio, metadata, mixes/processed versions, and process parameters where available.
requiredFollowUps: Verify current downloadable catalog.
Confirm item-level license terms.
Screen file consistency and process-parameter availability.

candidates.name: Slakh2100

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Mono synthetic material; stereo use must be explicitly labeled synthetic.

candidates.labelingType: Synthetic sources, mixtures, aligned MIDI, and metadata.
requiredFollowUps: Exclude known duplicate MIDI omitted tracks.
Label all stereo conversions as synthetic.
Do not use as professional mastering evidence.

candidates.name: MTG-Jamendo
candidates.scaleSummary: 55k+ full tracks with metadata and provided splits.
candidates.labelingType: Genre, instrument, mood/theme tags, artist metadata, and split files.
requiredFollowUps: Capture item-level licenses from audio license files.
Use as reference distribution only unless degraded pairs are generated.
Obtain separate authorization before commercial use.

candidates.name: Free Music Archive Dataset
candidates.scaleSummary: 106,574 tracks; 917 GiB full set; 30-second and full-length subsets.

audioFormat.encoding: MP3 in common subsets; full set requires quality screening

candidates.labelingType: Genre taxonomy, metadata, and extracted features.
requiredFollowUps: Filter by item-level audio license.
Screen for codec artifacts and low-quality sources.
Split by artist and album.

candidates.name: Open Dataset of Audio Quality
candidates.scaleSummary: Original release reports 240 stereo samples and 26 expert ratings per sample; expanded work reports 10,080 scores.
candidates.labelingType: Subjective audio-quality ratings and processed/reference examples.
requiredFollowUps: Reserve for metric calibration only.
Do not include in model training manifests.
Record release-specific license and audio checksums.

candidates.name: Artificial Audio Multitracks
candidates.scaleSummary: 3,000 artificial multitrack music pieces with precise annotations.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Synthetic corpus; dataset license must be verified separately from article license.

candidates.labelingType: Synthetic multitracks, mix track, and annotation-derived labels.
requiredFollowUps: Verify dataset license and access path.
Keep excluded from real-music quality evidence.
Label all results as synthetic pretraining or pipeline tests.

candidates.name: Commercial Stem and Multitrack Stores
candidates.scaleSummary: Potentially high-quality and variable scale, but ordinary access is account-bound and contract-limited.

audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Do not ingest without negotiated ML/data rights.

candidates.labelingType: Commercial stems or multitracks, often without dataset redistribution/training rights.
requiredFollowUps: Obtain custom written license covering ML training and derived artifacts.
Confirm redistribution, retention, and attribution obligations.
Do not ingest under ordinary consumer terms.

candidates.name: Streaming Services, Public Uploads, and Leaked Stems

audioFormat.channels: mastered audio or unofficial extracted stems
audioFormat.alignmentNotes: Rejected; no ingestion or derivative dataset construction.

candidates.labelingType: No defensible training labels or provenance.
requiredFollowUps: Exclude from all training, validation, and benchmarking.
Document rejection if proposed again.
candidates.notes: Representative terms reference is included to show that ordinary account-bound access is not a dataset training license.
